text.title: spec-example-ecommerce-cart Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for spec-example-ecommerce-cart as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Agent copies story-point estimates (5 pts, 3 pts) from this example into new specs.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: SDD replaces story points with token estimates. Explicitly instruct agents to remove story points and replace with token estimates per the 100k rule.

failure-mode.description: Agent treats the TypeScript interface in the Appendix as the canonical data model.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The interface is labeled preliminary. Without this label, agents use it in design.md as approved, bypassing the architectural decision process.

failure-mode.description: Agent copies NFR targets (200ms p95, 50k concurrent) verbatim into new specs.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: These values have no measurement source. Add a required [SOURCE] tag next to each NFR target when adapting to force replacement with real measurements.

failure-mode.description: Agent expands personas (Busy Parent Sarah, Deal Hunter Mike) with hallucinated pain points.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Persona pain points must come from real user research. Agents generate plausible-sounding but fabricated persona context. Provide real research or use placeholder text explicitly.
